creator: jacula@gmail.com
text:
Hi all.

I don't want to be too much prolix in my PR about wxHaskell, because it isn't
in need of a presentation and in any way this set of ports is enough rich of
comments and descriptions.
wxHaskell is a portable GUI library for Haskell that uses wxWidgets.
At present, it works with wxWidgets 2.6 and it's made up of two libraries:
the wxcore library and the wx library.
The wxcore library provides the core interface to the wxWidgets API and the wx
library, built on top of the first, provides useful features for programmers.

This set of ports is composed by:

ports:
x11-toolkits/hs-wxcore
x11-toolkits/hs-wx
x11-toolkits/hs-wxhaskell
x11-toolkits/hs-wxhaskell-docs
x11-toolkits/hs-xtc

files:
x11-toolkits/hs-wxhaskell/bsd.haskell.mk
x11-toolkits/hs-wxhaskell/bsd.wxhaskell.mk

x11-toolkits/hs-wxcore and x11-toolkits/hs-wx install, respectively, the two
Cabal distributions "wxcore" and "wx"; in addition they can install their own
library documentation built by haddock and HsColour.

x11-toolkits/hs-wxhaskell-docs installs examples, buggy sources, and the
hyperlinked documentation for the wxHaskell libraries both WXCore and WX,
already into the x11-toolkits/hs-wxcore and x11-toolkits/hs-wx ports, but
combined in only one index and directory.

x11-toolkits/hs-wxhaskell is a meta port, but it's also the joint point
between the documentation installed by the three ports x11-toolkits/hs-wx,
x11-toolkits/hs-wxcore and x11-toolkits/hs-wxhaskell-docs.
I like to add that a "wxhaskell" Cabal package doesn't exist, then also if
making a dummy package should be possible, this is useless if the other Cabal
distributions look only for wx and wxcore; actually, for the same reason, it
is useless also the wxhaskell.haddock interface, installed by the
x11-toolkits/hs-wxhaskell-docs port.

x11-toolkits/hs-xtc installs the Cabal distribution "xtc" that provides, as a
library, a typed interface to several wxHaskell controls.

x11-toolkits/hs-wxhaskell/bsd.haskell.mk is an attempt of standardization for
the Haskell related ports. It contains some variable definitions, macros and
targets that are supposed to make easier dealing with ports related to the
Haskell language.
The file includes a quotation from Petronius Arbiter, one of my favourite
author ;-), and I hope this inspires a smile in your reviews :-), because, in
fact, the implementation of my idea is not so simple and short as I should have
liked it was and someone couldn't like it:

 Quid me constricta spectatis fronte Catones,
   damnatisque novae simplicitatis opus?

x11-toolkits/hs-wxhaskell/bsd.wxhaskell.mk is a try of standardization for
the wxHaskell related ports. It includes some variable definitions that are
supposed to make easier dealing with ports related to the wxHaskell port set.

You have to know that the documentation structure of this set of wxHaskell
ports constitutes a unicum, built exclusively for FreeBSD, and is not
standard both for the form and for the contents; for example it contains the
HsColour files for the sources and it's possible to link the hyperlinkable
documentation of other distributions to the unified documentation for wx and
wxcore, skipping the problem of uselessness of the interface wxhaskell.haddock.
Furthermore this port set of wxHaskell has some limitation because the set of
x11-toolkits/wxgtk26* ports doesn't activate some configuration options like
"--enable-mediactrl" and "--with-odbc" then it will be not able to use the
classes wxMediaCtrl, IODBC and wxODBC; in any way it's not too much distant
the wxWidgets 2.8 age for wxHaskell.

On the whole I'm not satisfied with my work. In fact although my ideas found
an implementation, these are hidden by the necessities of the "make" syntax
and actually this means a difficulty of maintenance, scalability and debugging;
so I can fully understand if, at the end, someone decides to refuse this PR.
Naturally this could be the consequence of my limitation in using a so elegant
tool like "make" or of the inconsistency of my project. But in any way this is
my proposal and I hope that it is accepted.

Note also that I'm not a genius from Mars technique (but certainly a nerd <:^"])
then although the debugging was a very, (but very! :-( ) long process, bugs
should be possible and I hope in your help and sympathy.

From a FreeBSD hacker for the FreeBSD hackers.
From a haskeller for the haskellers.

Giuseppe Pilichi aka Jacula Modyun <jacula@gmail.com>

XAPI (in WxcClasses). Furthermore, it provides some useful
Xabstractions for the Haskell programmer. However, the library
Xjust uses functional abstraction: no overloading, new monads, or
Xother fanciness.

XThe wxHaskell libraries are distributed under the wxWindows

XThe wxWindows library licence is essentially the L-GPL, with
Xan exception stating that derived works in binary form may
Xbe distributed on the user's own terms.
X
XAuthor:         Daan Leijen

XThis port installs examples, buggy sources, and, if the option
XWITH_DOCS is set, installs also the hyperlinked documentation
Xfor the wxHaskell libraries both WXCore and WX, already into the
Xx11-toolkits/hs-wxcore and x11-toolkits/hs-wx ports, but combined
Xin only one index and directory. This last documentation is
Xgenerated by HsColour and haddock.
XThe unified documentation is inclusive of two haddock interface
Xfiles for WX and WXCore, suited at this port; to use these
Xinterfaces you have to reregister both the packages with the
Xhaddock fields pointing to them.
XRegistration files for this work can be obtained with installing
Xthe x11-toolkits/hs-wxhaskell port, defining WITH_REGISTERS;
Xdefining also WITH_REGISTRATION, this last port registers
Xautomatically WX and WXCore with the two new haddock interfaces.
XThese two options are the default ones for hs-wxhaskell.
END-of-hs-wxhaskell-docs/pkg-descr

XPORTNAME=	wxhaskell-docs

XMASTER_SITE_SUBDIR=	wxhaskell

X

X.endif
X
Xpost-install:
X.if !defined(WITHOUT_DOCS)
X	${LN} -s ${WXCORE_DOCSDIR}/html ${HADDOCK_HTML}/wxcore
X	${LN} -s ${WX_DOCSDIR}/html ${HADDOCK_HTML}/wx
X.endif
X
X.include "${.CURDIR}/../hs-wxhaskell/bsd.haskell.mk"
X.include <bsd.port.post.mk>
